prompt: I'm launching a B2B SaaS product for small law firms that automates client intake forms and document collection. I need a go-to-market strategy for the first 6 months post-launch. Budget is around $15k for marketing. We have 3 pilot customers but no paying customers yet. Target is 20 paying customers by month 6. Help me build this out properly.

assertions.text: Agent classifies this as Complex and runs full RPIRT rather than immediately writing a GTM plan
Research phase covers domain-relevant topics: legal tech market, B2B SaaS GTM, law firm buyer behavior
Review rubric criteria are business-oriented (Market Sizing, Channel Fit, Budget Realism, Measurability) not coding-oriented
Final GTM plan includes specific budget allocations (numbers) and measurable monthly milestones -- not just generic advice
Skill uses business vocabulary throughout (strategy, channels, milestones) rather than coding vocabulary (implement, deploy)
